title: Boat Hire in Cannes France: 2026 Costs, Choices & Marinas
html:
Hiring a boat in Cannes France opens up the glamorous Bay of Cannes and the Lérins Islands, with a motorboat for six starting from around €350 for a half-day. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators for a seamless experience.

Where to set off in Cannes France

Your departure point in Cannes is typically Vieux Port or Port Pierre Canto, both central and well-equipped. Vieux Port de Cannes lies at the heart of the city, close to the Palais des Festivals. Port Pierre Canto, a short walk east, offers more deep-water berths and direct access to the open sea. From either, you can head to the Îles de Lérins: Île Sainte-Marguerite and Île Saint-Honorat, each about a nautical mile offshore. The nearby town of Juan-les-Pins is a popular lunch stop, with its sandy beaches and clear waters. For a longer cruise, the Massif de l'Estérel and the bay of Theoule-sur-Mer are within easy reach.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Cannes France?

For smaller motorboats under a certain power, no licence is required in France, but the operator confirms exact requirements. Many hire companies accept an ICC (International Certificate of Competence) or a national driving licence for larger vessels. Always check with the operator when booking through BoatHire24; they will specify what documents you need and whether a brief sea trial is included. For bareboat charters of sailing yachts over a certain length, a licence or proof of experience is usually mandatory.

A local tip: moor early at Île Sainte-Marguerite to secure a spot in the public anchorage off the Fort Royal, especially during the high season from July to August when the Mistral wind can pick up.

Which boat to rent

  1. Azimut motor yacht, Luxury cruising for up to 10 guests, from €1,200 for a half-day.
  2. Fairline Targa 43, Sporty and comfortable, perfect for a day trip to the Lérins Islands, from €800 for four hours.
  3. Lagoon catamaran, Stable and spacious, ideal for 8, 12 guests on a full-day sail, from €1,500.
  4. Axopar 37, Versatile day cruiser for 8 guests, from €500 for a half-day; great for exploring the Estérel.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cannes France?

Prices vary widely, but a half-day motorboat for six starts at around €350 and a crewed yacht can exceed €2,500. Use the yacht charter Cannes France page on BoatHire24 to compare deals. Here is a typical price breakdown:

  • Shared tour (2, 3 hours): from €90 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (up to 6 guests): from €350.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): from €1,500.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (full-day, 10+ guests): from €2,500.

Season and weather

The peak season runs from June to September, with July and August being the warmest and busiest. The Mistral wind can blow from the north in spring and autumn, creating choppy seas in the bay. Calm mornings are best for crossing to the islands. In 2026, consider May or late September for quieter waters and more availability. Always consult the local forecast before heading out.

At a glance

  • Price band: €350, €2,500 per half or full day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full-day
  • Guest capacity: 4, 12 guests typical
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Cannes France

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Cannes?

Yes, many operators offer licence-free rental for small motorboats with limited engine power; always check the specific terms.

What islands can I visit from Cannes?

The Lérins Islands, Île Sainte-Marguerite and Île Saint-Honorat, are the top destinations, just 15 minutes from port.

Is it safe to sail in the Bay of Cannes?

Yes, the bay is generally calm, but watch for the Mistral wind and busy summer traffic; hire a skipper if unsure.

How far is Monaco from Cannes by boat?

Monaco is about 15 nautical miles east, roughly a 45-minute cruise at planing speed.
